questions.question: What is the syntax for two-way data binding in Angular (the 'banana in a box')?
An Angular template has <button [disabled]='isLoading'>Submit</button>. What does [disabled] do?
options: Adds a CSS class named 'disabled' when isLoading is true
Binds the DOM disabled property to the TypeScript isLoading expression — disables the button when true
Removes the button from the DOM when isLoading is false
Calls a method named 'disabled' on click

questions.question: What module must be imported to use [(ngModel)] in a component's template?
In an event handler receiving $event from (input), what type should you cast event.target to?
What is the difference between (click)='increment()' and [click]='increment()'?
options: They are identical — both bind the click event
(click)='increment()' binds an event handler; [click]='increment()' is invalid — [] is for property binding, not events
[click] fires once; (click) fires on every click
